# wraith

Wraith is a local-first developer tool for API mocking from recorded traffic. It records real API calls, synthesizes a deterministic local twin, and verifies the twin against the original recordings before tests, demos, or agents rely on it.

## Canonical URLs

- Homepage: https://wraith.cx/
- Quickstart: https://wraith.cx/quickstart/
- Installation: https://wraith.cx/installation/
- Twin lifecycle: https://wraith.cx/twin-lifecycle/
- Streaming APIs: https://wraith.cx/streaming/
- Simulation: https://wraith.cx/simulation/
- OpenAPI seed mode: https://wraith.cx/openapi/
- Configuration: https://wraith.cx/configuration/
- Changelog: https://wraith.cx/changelog/
- Privacy: https://wraith.cx/privacy/
- Security: https://wraith.cx/security/
- Contact: https://wraith.cx/contact/
- Email: info@wraith.cx
- Built by Bob: https://bob.cx/

## Install

```sh
curl -fsSL https://wraith.cx/install.sh | sh
```

Use `WRAITH_VERSION` to pin a public release.

## Current release

Latest documented release: v0.5.2, published 2026-05-01.

## License

Wraith release binaries are distributed under Elastic License 2.0.

## Core claims

- Record real API traffic through a local proxy.
- Scrub secrets before recordings hit disk.
- Synthesize deterministic, stateful local API twins.
- Check conformance against original recordings.
- Serve local twins for CI, local development, demos, and agent environments.
- Supports REST, GraphQL, gRPC unary/server-streaming, and SSE. WebSocket is not first-class yet.
